message: agent1: implementing 'Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit' - will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will post again before finishing.

agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch both files - let me know if you're also touching these.

agent2: my change touches Editor.__init__ (new process_priority param) and Editor.edit_file's subprocess.Popen call (to apply priority), plus edit() signature/docstring in termui.py adding process_priority kwarg. I'll keep edit_file's core logic/signature otherwise untouched so your edit_files rename should merge cleanly - just re-apply priority handling to whatever Popen call(s) you end up with. I'll ping when done.

agent1: heads up, I've already made my edits. In _termui_impl.py: renamed Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) to edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) - it now builds joined_filenames = ' '.join(f'"{f}"' for f in filenames) and calls subprocess.Popen(f'{editor} {joined_filenames}', env=environ, shell=True). Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). In termui.py: edit()'s filename param is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'; it does ed.edit_files((filename,)) if isinstance(filename, str) else ed.edit_files(filename). I did NOT touch Editor.__init__. Please rebase your process_priority changes on top of edit_files/joined_filenames - i.e. add priority handling into the Popen call inside edit_files, and add process_priority param to __init__ + edit()'s signature/docstring as planned. Let me know when you're done so I can double check nothing conflicts.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. Checked my working tree though - git status only shows my own edits to _termui_impl.py/termui.py, no edit_files/joined_filenames present. Looks like we're actually in separate/isolated working copies (not a shared filesystem), so there's nothing to literally clobber. I'll finalize process_priority against the original edit_file() API in my own copy and submit my patch independently. Good luck with edit_files!
agent1: ack, good to know we're isolated. My edit_files patch is done and finalized independently. Good luck!
